method: Re-ran identical 8-task token-cost benchmark from token-cost-bench-2026-04-25-postfix4.json after M0.5 + M1 packet + M2 compact Codex skill + M3 LIVE enrichment + M4a partial all landed (commit ec3c65b). Pre-bench gates: ensureFresh({force:true}) -> nodes=1637 edges=4515 unresolved=2571; graph_packet(feature:freshness) -> 358 chars, all required sections present except CONTRACTS (none in overlay for this feature); graph_packet(feature:freshness, live:true) -> 569 chars with 'LIVE: enriched (100ms)' + LAST TOUCHED block. All three gates passed. Big change tested: ORIENT/PLAN/IMPACT could now reach for graph_packet first.

regression_vs_postfix4.note: Slight upward drift in unresolved-edge count after M0.5+M1+M2+M3+M4a churn. Trust gate still 'ok'.

tasks.prompt: What is this repo about and what are its main exports?

graph.notes: Unchanged. graph_packet does not apply to a whole-repo orient question (it requires a feature/task target).

tasks.packet_applicability: no — packet is feature/task scoped, not repo-level; brief.agent.md remains correct first call

tasks.prompt: How does graph_consequences work end-to-end? Trace the call flow.
Who calls openExistingDb and from which files?
What breaks if I refactor expandClassRollupTargets?

graph.notes: Unchanged. Tried graph_packet(target='expandClassRollupTargets') -> ERROR: target not found as feature or task. Packet is overlay-scoped and refuses raw symbols. To use packet here the agent must reframe to feature:query-verbs, which loses symbol-level fidelity (no tests_adjacent for the specific function, no risk_flags). Net: graph_consequences still wins on impact for symbol targets; packet does NOT substitute.

tasks.packet_applicability: no for symbol targets — feature reframe loses fidelity; consequences remains the right verb

tasks.prompt: I want to change ensureFresh — read order + risk?

graph.notes: Unchanged. Tried graph_packet(target='ensureFresh') -> ERROR: not found as feature/task. Reframe to feature:freshness gives 358-char packet but loses the SIGNALS line, source-occurrence file list (24 files), and READ ORDER ranked by caller depth — all of which the planner needs. graph_change_plan remains the right call for symbol-level planning.

tasks.packet_applicability: no for symbol targets — feature reframe loses the SIGNALS line and ranked READ ORDER

tasks.prompt: What features touch mcp/stdio/freshness/orchestrator.js and what tests cover them?

graph.notes: PACKET WIN. graph_packet(feature:freshness, live=true) returns 569 chars vs 1200 for graph_pull. Token est drops 1200 -> 1050 (-12.5%). Quality unchanged at 4 (still no test attribution because overlay has tests 0/8 — same gap as postfix4, packet cannot synthesize what overlay lacks). LIVE block adds last-touched commits which compensates partially. delta_vs_postfix4: -150 tokens.

tasks.packet_applicability: yes — feature target, packet shape is well-suited

tasks.prompt: Is the graph fresh and trustworthy right now?

graph.notes: Slightly heavier output: now includes overlayQuality block (tests 0/8, docs 0/8, deps 7/8, related 1/8) plus dirtySeams. trust=ok still. unresolvedEdges=2571 (was 2473). +20 tokens for richer output, still the cheapest answer.

tasks.prompt: Why does graph_impact return so many edges in some cases?

graph.notes: Unchanged self-introspection limitation; not addressed by M0.5-M4a.

summary.notes: Win count holds at 6-2. Token delta improves modestly (-17.3% -> -21.0%, +3.7pp better) entirely from the cross_layer task where graph_packet substitutes for graph_pull. Quality delta unchanged at -0.25 (DEBUG self-introspection still the lone drag). graph_packet does NOT improve ORIENT/PLAN/IMPACT in practice because packet only resolves feature: and task: targets — raw symbols error out, and reframing to a parent feature loses symbol-level fidelity (SIGNALS line, ranked READ ORDER, tests_adjacent for the specific function, risk_flags). Packet is genuinely useful only when the question is already feature-shaped (cross_layer).

packet_substitution_finding.expected: graph_packet replaces graph_consequences + graph_pull on ORIENT/PLAN/IMPACT
packet_substitution_finding.observed: Substitution only works for cross_layer (feature-shaped question). ORIENT is repo-level (brief.agent.md still correct first call). PLAN and IMPACT in the 8-task suite use raw symbol names ('ensureFresh', 'expandClassRollupTargets'); graph_packet rejects these with ERROR: target not found as feature or task in overlay/tasks.

feature_reframe_attempted.plan_ensureFresh_via_feature_freshness: loses SIGNALS line (5 callers, 6 deps, 0 tests, 24 source-occurrence files), loses ranked READ ORDER, loses RISK CONFIRM verdict
feature_reframe_attempted.impact_expandClassRollupTargets_via_feat: loses tests_adjacent (correctly returns the specific test file from postfix4 fix), loses risk_flags, loses last_touched commits at function granularity

packet_substitution_finding.recommendation: Either (a) make graph_packet accept symbol targets and synthesize a packet from graph_consequences-style data, OR (b) update the skill so agents only use graph_packet for feature:/task: targets and continue to use graph_change_plan / graph_consequences for symbols. (b) is the cheaper fix and matches the verbs' actual contracts.

new_bugs_surfaced.impact: M3 LIVE enrichment fails on this specific feature. Worked on feature:freshness. Suggests live enrichment path has a feature-specific code path that returns non-JSON for some inputs.

new_bugs_surfaced.where: graph_packet SNAPSHOT line vs graph_health() trust field
new_bugs_surfaced.observed: Packet prints 'trust=weak' in SNAPSHOT line, but graph_health returns trust='ok' for the same snapshot (1468 trust-relevant unresolved is below the OK threshold). Packet is using a different (or older) trust calculation.
new_bugs_surfaced.impact: Confusing signal — agent reads packet, thinks trust is weak, and downgrades verb confidence unnecessarily.

new_bugs_surfaced.observed: briefStaleVsManifest=true AND unresolvedCategorizationStaleVsManifest=true immediately after a force rebuild.
new_bugs_surfaced.impact: Force rebuild does not regenerate briefs/categorization in the same pass. Agent following the skill will be told to 'regenerate with graph-brief.mjs' even though they just rebuilt.

new_bugs_surfaced.observed: 0/8 features have tests[] populated even though tests/unit/* IMPORTS edges (146) landed in postfix4. Overlay tests[] vs IMPORTS edges are tracked separately and the gap was not closed in M0.5-M4a.
new_bugs_surfaced.impact: Map-quality signal still says 'tests 0/8' which may discourage planners from trusting test attribution even when IMPORTS coverage is now there.

new_bugs_surfaced.observed: Output missing CONTRACTS section that the skill's M1 spec calls for. Reason: feature has no contracts in overlay. Packet silently omits the section instead of rendering 'CONTRACTS: none'.
new_bugs_surfaced.impact: Agent following expected-section list cannot tell whether packet broke or feature truly has no contracts.

recommendation: M0.5+M1+M2+M3+M4a delivered a measurable token improvement (-17.3% -> -21.0%) but only on the cross_layer task. The headline assumption that graph_packet would help ORIENT/PLAN/IMPACT did not hold because packet only resolves feature/task targets, and the bench's PLAN/IMPACT prompts use raw symbol names. Highest-leverage next moves: (1) extend graph_packet to accept symbol targets (synthesize from graph_consequences); (2) reconcile packet's SNAPSHOT trust calc with graph_health's trust gate; (3) make ensureFresh({force:true}) regenerate briefs + categorization in the same transaction so freshness flags are consistent post-rebuild; (4) fix the LIVE-non-JSON packet bug surfaced on feature:query-verbs.
